author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

source.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But Thomas, one of the twelve, called Didymus, was not with them when Jesus came.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The other disciples therefore said unto him, We have seen the Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But he said, Except I shall see in His hands —I will not believe.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As to believe carelessly and in a random way, comes of an over-easy temper; so to be beyond measure curious and meddlesome, marks a most gross understanding.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account Thomas is held to blame.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he believed not the Apostles when they said, “We have seen the Lord”; not so much mistrusting them, as deeming the thing to be impossible, that is to say, the resurrection from the dead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since he saith not, “I do not believe you,” but, “Except I put my hand—I do not believe.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But how was it, that when all were collected together, he alone was absent?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Probably after the dispersion which had lately taken place, he had not returned even then.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But do thou, when thou seest the unbelief of the disciple, consider the lovingkindness of the Lord, how for the sake of a single soul He showed Himself with His wounds, and cometh in order to save even the one, though he was grosser than the rest; on which account indeed he sought proof from the grossest of the senses, and would not even trust his eyes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he said not, “Except I see,” but, “Except I handle,” he saith, lest what he saw might somehow be an apparition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet the disciples who told him these things, were at the time worthy of credit, and so was He that promised; yet, since he desired more, Christ did not deprive him even of this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And why doth He not appear to him straightway, instead of “after eight days”? ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In order that being in the mean time continually instructed by the disciples, and hearing the same thing, he might be inflamed to more eager desire, and be more ready to believe for the future.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But whence knew he that His side had been opened?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From having heard it from the disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then did he believe partly, and partly not believe?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because this thing was very strange and wonderful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But observe, I pray you, the truthfulness of the disciples, how they hide no faults, either their own or others’, but record them with great veracity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Jesus again presenteth himself to them, and waiteth not to be requested by Thomas, nor to hear any such thing, but before he had spoken, Himself prevented him, and fulfilled his desire; showing that even when he spake those words to the disciples, He was present.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He used the same words, and in a manner conveying a sharp rebuke, and instruction for the future.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For having said,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Reach hither thy finger, and behold My hands; and reach hither thy hand, and thrust it into My side”; He added,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And be not faithless, but believing.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that his doubt proceeded from unbelief?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But it was before he had received the Spirit; after that, it was no longer so, but, for the future, they were perfected.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And not in this way only did Jesus rebuke him, but also by what follows; for when he, being fully satisfied, breathed again, and cried aloud,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “My Lord, and my God,” He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Because thou hast seen Me, thou hast believed; blessed are they who have not seen, and yet have believed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this is of faith, to receive things not seen; since,“Faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.” ( Heb. xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And here He pronounceth blessed not the disciples only, but those also who after them should believe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Yet,” saith some one, “the disciples saw and believed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yes, but they sought nothing of the kind, but from the proof of the napkins, they straightway received the word concerning the Resurrection, and before they saw the body, exhibited all faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When therefore any one in the present day say, “I would that I had lived in those times, and had seen Christ working miracles,” let them reflect, that, “Blessed are they who have not seen, and yet have believed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is worth enquiring, how an incorruptible body showed the prints of the nails, and was tangible by a mortal hand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But be not thou disturbed; what took place was a matter of condescension.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that which was so subtle and light as to enter in when the doors were shut, was free from all density ; but this marvel was shown, that the Resurrection might be believed, and that men might know that it was the Crucified One Himself, and that another rose not in His stead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account He arose bearing the signs of the Cross, and on this account He eateth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At least the Apostles everywhere made this a sign of the Resurrection, saying, “We, who did eat and drink with Him.” ( Acts x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As therefore when we see Him walking on the waves before the Crucifixion, we do not say, that that body is of a different nature, but of our own; so after the Resurrection, when we see Him with the prints of the nails, we will no more say, that he is therefore corruptible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He exhibited these appearances on account of the disciple.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And many other signs truly did Jesus.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since this Evangelist hath mentioned fewer than the others, he tells us that neither have all the others mentioned them all, but as many as were sufficient to draw the hearers to belief.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, “If,” it saith, “they should be written every one, I suppose that even the world itself could not contain the books.” ( c. xxi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whence it is clear, that What they have mentioned they wrote not for display, but only for the sake of what was useful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For how could they who omitted the greater part, write these others for display?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why went they not through them all?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Chiefly on account of their number; besides, they also considered, that he who believed not those they had mentioned, would not give heed to a greater number; while he who received these, would have no need of another in order to believe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And here too he seems to me to be for the time speaking of the miracles after the Resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “In the presence of His disciples.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as before the Resurrection it was necessary that many should be done, in order that they might believe that He was the Son of God, so was it also after the Resurrection, in order that they might admit that He had arisen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For another reason also he has added, “In the presence of His disciples,” because He conversed with them alone after the Resurrection; wherefore also He said, “The world seeth Me no more.” ( c. xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then, in order that thou mayest understand that what was done was done only for the sake of the disciples, he added,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That believing ye might have life in His Name.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Speaking generally to mankind, and showing that not on Him who is believed on, but on ourselves, he bestows a very great favor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “In His Name,” that is, “through Him”; for He is the Life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Chap. xxi. ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “After these things, Jesus showed Himself again to the disciples at the sea of Tiberias.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that He remaineth not with them continually, nor as before?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He appeared, for instance, in the evening, and flew away; then after eight days again once, and again flew away; then after these things by the sea, and again with great terror.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what is the, “showed”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From this it is clear that He was not seen unless He condescended, because His body was henceforth incorruptible, and of unmixed purity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But wherefore hath the writer mentioned the place?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To show that he had now taken away the greater part of their fear, so that they now ventured forth from their dwelling, and went about everywhere.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they were no longer shut up at home, but had gone into Galilee, avoiding the danger from the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Simon, therefore, comes to fish.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since neither was He with them continually, nor was the Spirit yet given, nor they at that time yet entrusted with anything, having nothing to do, they went after their trade.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “There were together Simon Peter, and Thomas, and Nathanael,” (he that was called by Philip,) “and the sons of Zebedee, and two others.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Having then nothing to do, they went to their fishing, and this same they did by night, because they were greatly afraid.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This Luke also mentions; but this is not the same occasion, but a different one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the other disciples followed, because they were henceforth bound to one another, and at the same time desired to see the fishing, and to bestow their leisure well.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As they then were laboring and wearied, Jesus presenteth Himself before them, and doth not at once reveal Himself, so that they enter into converse with Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He therefore saith to them,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Have ye any meat ?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For a time He speaketh rather after a human manner, as if about to buy somewhat of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when they made signs that they had none, He bade them cast their nets to the right; and on casting they obtained a haul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when they recognized Him, the disciples Peter and John again exhibited the peculiarities of their several tempers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The one was more fervent, the other more lofty; the one more keen, the other more clear-sighted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account John first recognized Jesus, Peter first came to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For no ordinary signs were they which had taken place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What were they?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: First, that so many fish were caught; then, that the net did not break; then, that before they landed, the coals had been found, and fish laid thereon, and bread.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He no longer made things out of matter already subsisting, as, through a certain dispensation, He did before the Crucifixion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When therefore Peter knew Him, he threw down all, both fish and nets, and girded himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou his respect and love?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet they were only two hundred cubits off; but not even so could Peter wait to go to Him in the boat, but reached the shore by swimming.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then doth Jesus?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Come,” He saith, “dine.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And none of them durst ask Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they no longer had the same boldness, nor were they so confident, nor did they now approach Him with speech, but with silence and great fear and reverence, sat down giving heed to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For they knew that it was the Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore they did not ask Him, “Who art Thou?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But seeing that His form was altered, and full of much awfulness, they were greatly amazed, and desired to ask somewhat concerning it; but fear, and their knowledge that He was not some other, but the Same, checked the enquiry, and they only ate what He created for them with a greater exertion of power than before.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For here He no more looketh to heaven, nor performeth those human acts, showing that those also which He did were done by way of condescension.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And to show that He remained not with them continually, nor in like manner as before, It saith that,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “This was the third time that Jesus appeared to them, after that He arose from the dead.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He biddeth them “to bring of the fish,” to show that what they saw was no appearance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But here indeed it saith not that He ate with them, but Luke, in another place, saith that He did; for “He was eating together with them.” ( Acts i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the, “how,” it is not ours to say; for these things came to pass in too strange a manner, not as though His nature now needed food, but from an act of condescension, in proof of the Resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps when ye heard these things, ye glowed, and called those happy who were then with Him, and those who shall be with Him at the day of the general Resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then use every exertion that we may see that admirable Face.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if when now we hear we so burn, and desire to have been in those days which He spent upon earth, and to have heard His Voice, and seen His face, and to have approached, and touched, and ministered unto Him; consider how great a thing it is to see Him no longer in a mortal body, nor doing human actions, but with a body guard of Angels, being ourselves also in a form of unmixed purity, and beholding Him, and enjoying the rest of that bliss which passes all language.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, I entreat, let us use every means, so as not to miss such glory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For nothing is difficult if we be willing, nothing burdensome if we give heed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If we endure, we shall also reign with Him.” ( 2 Tim. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then is, “If we endure”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If we bear tribulations, if persecutions, if we walk in the strait way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the strait way is by its nature laborious, but by our will it is rendered light, from the hope of things to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For our present light affliction worketh for us a far more exceeding and eternal weight of glory; while we look not at the things which are seen, but at those which are not seen.” ( 2 Cor. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then transfer our eyes to heaven, and continually imagine “those” things, and behold them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if we always spend our time with them, we shall not be moved to desire the pleasures of this world, nor find it hard to bear its sorrows; but we shall laugh at these and the like, and nothing will be able to enslave or lift us up, if only we direct our longing thither, and look to that love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And why say I that we shall not grieve at present troubles?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We shall henceforth not even appear to see them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Such a thing is strong desire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Those, for instance, who are not at present with us, but being absent are loved, we image every day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For mighty is the sovereignty of love, it alienates the soul from all things else, and chains to the desired object.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If thus we love Christ, all things here will seem to be a shadow, an image, a dream.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We too shall say, “Who shall separate us from the love of Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Shall tribulation, or distress?” ( Rom. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not, “money, or wealth, or beauty,” (these are very mean and contemptible,) but he hath put the things which seem to be grievous, famines, persecutions, deaths.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He then spat on these even, as being nought; but we for the sake of money separate ourselves from our life, and cut ourselves off from the light.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And Paul indeed prefers “neither death, nor life, nor things present, nor things to come, nor any other creature,” to the love which is towards Him; but we, if we see a little portion of gold, are fired, and trample on His laws.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if these things are intolerable when spoken of, much more are they so when done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the terrible thing is this, that we shudder to hear, but do not shudder to do: we swear readily, and perjure ourselves, and plunder, and exact usury, care nothing for sobriety, desist from exactness in prayer, transgress most of the commandments, and for the sake of money make no account of our own members.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he that loves wealth will work ten thousand mischiefs to his neighbor, and to himself as well.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He will easily be angry with him, and revile him, and call him fool, and swear and perjure himself, and does not even preserve the measures of the old law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he that loves gold will not love his neighbor; yet we, for the Kingdom’s sake, are bidden to love even our enemies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now if by fulfilling the old commandments, we shall not be able to enter the Kingdom of heaven, unless our righteousness exceed and go beyond them, when we transgress even these, what excuse shall we obtain?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He that loves money, not only will not love his enemies, but will even treat his friends as enemies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why speak I of friends? the lovers of money have often ignored nature itself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Such a one knows not kindred, remembers not companionship, reverences not age, has no friend, but will be ill-disposed towards all, and above all others to himself, not only by destroying his soul, but by racking himself with ten thousand cares, and toils, and sorrows.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he will endure foreign travels, hatreds, dangers, plots, anything whatever, only that he may have in his house the root of all evil, and may count much gold.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then can be more grievous than this disease?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is void of any luxury or pleasure, for the sake of which men often sin, it is void of honor or glory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the lover of money suspects that he has tens of thousands, and really has many, who accuse, and envy, and slander, and plot against him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Those whom he has wronged hate him as having been ill-used; those who have not yet suffered, fearing least they may suffer, and sympathizing with those who have, manifest the same hostility; while the greater and more powerful, being stung and indignant on account of the humbler sort, and at the same time also envying him, are his enemies and haters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And why speak I of men?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when one hath God also made his enemy, what hope shall there then be for him? what consolation? what comfort?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He that loves riches will never be able to use them; he will be their slave and keeper, not their master.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, being ever anxious to make them more, he will never be willing to spend them; but he will cut short himself, and be in poorer state than any poor man, as nowhere stopping in his desire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet riches are made not that we should keep, but that we should use them; but if we are going to bury them for others, what can be more miserable than we, who run about desiring to get together the possessions of all men, that we may shut them up within, and cut them off from common use?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But there is another malady not less than this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Some men bury their money in the earth, others in their bellies, and in pleasure and drunkenness; together with injustice adding to themselves the punishment of wantonness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Some minister with their substance to parasites and flatterers, others to dice and harlots, others to different expenses of the same kind, cutting out for themselves ten thousand roads that lead to hell, but leaving the right and sanctioned road which leads to heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And yet it hath not greater gain only, but greater pleasure than the things we have mentioned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he who gives to harlots is ridiculous and shameful, and will have many quarrels, and brief pleasure; or rather, not even brief, because, give what he will to the women his mistresses, they will not thank him for it; for, “The house of a stranger is a cask with holes.” ( Prov. xxiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, that sort of persons is impudent, and Solomon hath compared their love to the grave; and then only do they stop, when they see their lover stripped of all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or rather, such a woman doth not stop even then, but tricks herself out the more, and tramples on him when he is down, and excites much laughter against him, and works him so much mischief, as it is not possible even to describe by words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not such is the pleasure of the saved; for neither hath any there a rival, but all rejoice and are glad, both they that receive blessings, and they that look on.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: No anger, no despondency, no shame, no disgrace, besiege the soul of such a one, but great is the gladness of his conscience, and great his hope of things to come; bright his glory, and great his distinction; and more than all is the favor and safety which is from God, and not one precipice, nor suspicion, but a waveless harbor, and calm.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Considering therefore all these things, and comparing pleasure with pleasure, let us choose the better, that we may obtain the good things to come, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and dominion for ever and ever.
